title: NQ Replenished Bid Launchpad

summary.thesis: A bid that keeps replenishing at the same price and survives repeated selling pressure can become the launchpad for a move into higher liquidity.
summary.operator_translation: The long only makes sense if the defended bid stays firm, sellers fail to break it cleanly, and initiative buying starts to return.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when the replay shows repeated same-price defense plus a credible upper target. Emphasize where the operator should not open if the defense is not yet confirmed.

when_relevant: A narrow bid zone keeps reappearing at the same price.
Sellers continue to hit the defended price but price does not continue lower.
Local structure starts to hold higher lows or reclaim micro pivots.
There is visible upper liquidity that can act as the next destination.
required_evidence: significant liquidity remains active near price
initiative buying or net delta shifts in favor of the defended side
price rejection from the defended price remains controlled
confirmation_signals: The defended bid survives another touch without structural breakdown.
Aggressive buyers begin to lift while the defended price still holds.
Price reclaims the nearest local pivot and starts traveling toward upper liquidity.
invalidation_signals: The defended price fails without immediate renewed bid support.
Replenishment disappears and price remains heavy below the launch area.
The move cannot reclaim the local pivot and slips back into balance.
no_trade_conditions: Do not buy in the middle of balance just because a single bid looks large.
Do not buy before the defended price proves it can survive repeated hits.
Do not buy if the larger session context is pressing directly into a stronger opposing region.

entry_archetypes.name: first reclaimed higher low
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the defended bid survives and price reclaims the most recent micro pivot.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Skip if the reclaim is weak and directly runs into overhead pressure.

entry_archetypes.name: pullback into defended bid
entry_archetypes.description: Enter on a pullback that retests the defended price and shows renewed buyer aggression.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Skip if the pullback becomes acceptance below the defended area.

management_notes: Expect shallow pullbacks if the launchpad is real.
Watch whether upper liquidity is consumed cleanly or only tapped and rejected.
After the first upper objective is harvested, reassess for post-harvest balance or reversal.
review_questions: Was the defended price actually defended multiple times or was it only one visible bid?
Did the operator enter before initiative returned?
Did the operator respect the larger-session context or focus only on the local defense?
What happened immediately after upper liquidity was tagged?
